data.title: JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon Says U.S. Should Stockpile Missiles, Not Bitcoin
data.description:
• JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon believes the U.S. should prioritize defense logistics over stockpiling bitcoin.
  • Dimon warned that the U.S. is only equipped with missiles for seven days in a potential war in the South China Sea.
  • Despite Dimon’s criticism of BTC, JPMorgan will soon allow clients to buy bitcoin.

data.title: TON Falls 7% as Sell-Off Tied to Musk's Dispute of Telegram, xAI Partnership Continues
data.description:
• Toncoin (TON) plummeted over 7% in the last 24 hours and around 17% after a partnership between Telegram and Elon Musk's xAI was denied by Musk.
  • Despite the denial, Telegram CEO Pavel Durov stated that the deal has been "agreed in principle" and that "formalities are pending."
  • The price of TON is now being closely watched, with key levels between $3.00 and $3.22, and a breakdown or breakout from this range could signal the next move.

data.title: XRP Down 4% as Global Economic Tensions Trigger Market Selloff
data.description:
• XRP fell 5.7% to $2.14 amid global economic tensions and market liquidations.
  • The U.S. Court of International Trade's decision to overturn tariffs contributed to market volatility.
  • XRP faced resistance at $2.21 and found support at $2.11, with consolidation suggesting potential stabilization.

data.title: Crypto Bulls Rack up $600M Liquidations as Bitcoin Drops Under $104K
data.description:
Bitcoin prices fell below $104,000, leading to over $600 million in liquidations, marking the highest losses since February.

U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff increase on Chinese steel and aluminum has rattled global trade markets, affecting crypto markets.

The broader crypto market experienced a sell-off, with major cryptocurrencies like Ether, XRP, and Solana dropping significantly.

data.title: Dogecoin Dives 8%, Pepe Down 12% in Weekend Crypto Sell-Off
data.description:
• Cryptocurrencies experienced significant losses over the weekend, with Dogecoin dropping over 8% and Pepe falling 12%.
  • Bitcoin's price fell over 2%, trading just above $103,600, while the CoinDesk 20 index decreased by 4.2%.
  • Analysts link the downturn to renewed U.S.-China trade tensions, with market volatility expected to continue.

data.title: FTX Repayments May Have Positive Market Impact: Coinbase
data.description:
• The FTX Recovery Trust is expected to return $5 billion to creditors this Friday.
  • The first repayment round in February sent $7 billion to smaller claimants.
  • The market mood is more optimistic now, potentially amplifying repayment impact.

data.title: Bitcoin Slips Below $104K, Cryptos Slide as U.S.-China Tariff Tensions Flare Up
data.description:
• Markets turned down on Friday due to renewed concerns over U.S.-China trade tensions flaring up.
  • Bitcoin fell 2.1% below $104,000, while the CoinDesk 20 index declined 4.2%.
  • Smart contract platforms and crypto stocks experienced significant declines, with Bitdeer down 8.3%.

data.title: AVAX Slides 7% Before Establishing Strong Resistance at $22.35 Level
data.description:
• AVAX dropped 6.78% over 24 hours, falling from $23.04 to $21.48 amid a broader crypto market decline.
  • Geopolitical tensions and uncertain trade policies continue to weigh on cryptocurrency markets, with bitcoin recently dropping below $106,000.

data.title: Trump Media Has $2.3B in Bitcoin Buying Power After Closing Capital Raise
data.description:
• Trump Media closed a $2.44 billion capital raise with funds planned to be sued to create a bitcoin (BTC) treasury.
  • The company sold $1.44 billion in common stock and $1 billion in convertible notes to some 50 institutional investors.
  • The move aims to make the company one of the largest publicly-traded U.S. holders of bitcoin.

data.title: Spot Bitcoin ETFs Broke 10-Day Inflow Streak With $358M of Outflows Thursday: JPMorgan
data.description:
• U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs recorded their first day of outflows in 10 trading sessions on Thursday, the report said.
  • The bank noted that spot bitcoin ETFs saw $358 million of redemptions yesterday.
  • BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) bucked the trend, with $125 million in net inflows, JPMorgan said.
